ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the employee */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry in which the employee works */,
  "hiring_date" TEXT /* The date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* The date the employee left the company (null if still employed) */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* The ethnicity of the employee */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* The nationality of the employee */,
  "disability_status" TEXT /* Whether the employee has a disability */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* The marital status of the employee */,
  "employee_status" TEXT /* Current employment status (e.g., active, on leave, terminated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_gender FOREIGN KEY ("gender") REFERENCES genders ("gender_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_education FOREIGN KEY ("education") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_industry FOREIGN KEY ("industry") REFERENCES industries ("industry_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_records" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee associated with the salary record */,
  "industry_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience in the industry */,
  "salary" REAL /* Annual salary of the employee */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* The date the salary became effective */,
  "currency" TEXT /* The currency in which the salary is paid */,
  "bonus" REAL /* Annual bonus amount */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Description of employee benefits (e.g., health insurance, retirement plan) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_records_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary in the industry */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the industry */,
  "number_of_employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level (e.g., high school, bachelor's) */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Specific degree or certification (e.g., Bachelor of Science, MBA) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  "pronouns" TEXT /* Preferred pronouns (e.g., he/him, she/her, they/them) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee providing the feedback */,
  "satisfaction_rating" INTEGER /* Satisfaction rating given by the employee (scale of 1-5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the employee */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* The date the feedback was provided */,
  "suggestions" TEXT /* Suggestions for improvement provided by the employee */,
  "improvement_rating" INTEGER /* Rating for areas of improvement (scale of 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_feedback_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee associated with this role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role the employee holds */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the role */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the role (null if current) */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department in which the role is located */,
  "location" TEXT /* The location of the role (e.g., office, remote) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* The employee ID of the manager */,
  "performance_rating" INTEGER /* Performance rating for the role (scale of 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
